reviews.review_msg: Amazing professor and person, genuinely no complaints.

Justin is a really good professor as he explains stuff really well. The class on race and crime was really eye openining and interesting. The exams are really easy because he always tells which readings we will be tested on ! I would highly recommend taking a class with him !
He is the most attentive and caring professor.

I loved Justin's class, it has been one of my favourite classes I've ever taken at SFU. He posts the lectures slides, however you need to attend lecture, as the slides are a little bare. But, the lectures are extremely interesting and engaging. He is super approachable and helpful when you have questions. TAKE THE COURSE ITS SO GOOD

Justin is one of the best professors I've had during my time at SFU. He's smart, passionate, and is all around great at what he does. His lectures are engaging and interesting. Take the class, you won't regret it!

Justin is a wonderful professor. I'm only halfway through the semester with him but I felt as though he already deserves a good review. He does a fabulous job at actually teaching the material, so you won't feel as though you have to teach yourself when exams come around. He's clearly super passionate and well-versed in the content. Take his class!

If you have a chance to take one of his upper division seminar courses, seize the opportunity. He only grades on one paper and one presentation and he marks fairly easy provided that you write well and you capture the essence of the issues. Wonderful teacher.
Dr. Lysova is very passionate about the course content and is great at encouraging class discussion. Her knowledge of the subject is impressive! Very friendly. Great Professor!
Hard class but fair. Text is expensive and sometimes she rambles but Id totally take this again. Super good experience

Class itself was interesting but with the amount of work required for each little assignment, I would not recommend. This class required most of my time and effort while I had 3 other classes. I did better in the other 3 classes which were also upper level. Avoid unless you want to get the same feedback time after time.

Not a 'easy A' but straightforward about expectations and very helpful. Told us exactly what the exam would be and stuck to it. Good prof. No surprises. She was very understanding about the bad term I had and helped me out more than other profs did.

Do not take this class if you have a life or you're taking more than 3 classes in a semester. Readings are long and dry and bi-weekly reflection journals take a lot of time and effort for such a small % of grade. Her feedback is vague and she expects graduate + level writing from undergrads.  Paper are very hard. very hard to get a good grade.
Great prof and class. Would take again!
